questions.question: What is a constitution?

options.A: A set of basic rules for a country

questions.explanation: A constitution contains the basic rules according to which a country is governed.

questions.question: Which document contains the fundamental laws of India?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India is the supreme law of the country.

questions.question: When did the Constitution of India come into effect?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India came into effect on 26 January 1950.

questions.question: Who was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a constitution?

options.B: To define rules for governing a country

questions.explanation: A constitution defines the rules and structure of government.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key feature of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Equality is an important principle of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which body prepared the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly prepared the Constitution of India.

questions.question: What does equality before law mean?

options.A: Some people are above the law
options.B: Everyone is equal before the law

questions.explanation: Equality before law means all citizens are treated equally.

questions.question: Which system divides power between central and state governments?
questions.explanation: Federalism divides power between central and state governments.

questions.question: Which branch of government makes laws?
questions.explanation: The legislature is responsible for making laws.

questions.question: Which branch implements laws?
questions.explanation: The executive branch implements and enforces laws.

questions.question: Which branch interprets laws?
questions.explanation: The judiciary interprets laws and ensures justice.

questions.question: Which right ensures people are treated equally?
questions.explanation: The Right to Equality guarantees equal treatment under the law.

questions.question: Which principle means the state does not favor any religion?
questions.explanation: Secularism means the state treats all religions equally.

questions.question: Which of the following protects citizens' rights?
questions.explanation: The Constitution protects the rights of citizens.

questions.question: Which system ensures government is responsible to Parliament?
questions.explanation: In a parliamentary system the government is accountable to Parliament.

questions.question: What does democracy mean?
questions.explanation: Democracy means government of the people, by the people and for the people.

questions.question: Which institution protects the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The judiciary protects the Constitution and ensures laws follow it.

questions.question: Which principle ensures fairness in governance?
questions.explanation: Rule of law ensures that laws apply equally to everyone.

questions.question: Which body elects representatives to the government?
questions.explanation: Citizens elect representatives in a democratic system.

questions.question: What ensures balance among branches of government?
questions.explanation: Separation of powers prevents any branch from becoming too powerful.

questions.question: What is the highest law of India?
questions.explanation: The Constitution is the supreme law of India.

questions.question: Which value promotes unity in diversity?
questions.explanation: Secularism allows people of all religions to live together peacefully.

questions.question: Which institution passes laws in India?
questions.explanation: Parliament is responsible for making laws.

questions.question: Why is the Constitution important?
questions.explanation: The Constitution provides a framework for governing the country.
